specs.rationale: Provides comprehensive index of all Mamba specs organized by component; essential for understanding which stdlib modules need conformance coverage
Core spec for this change - directly defines P0/P1 conformance test requirements, golden file approach, CPython 3.12 reference, and test categories for data structures, exceptions, generators
General conformance testing framework spec - defines xfail handling, fixture structure, golden file format, and conformance test harness architecture
Test infrastructure spec - defines fixture discovery, RUN directives, CPython fixture integration, and test execution flow used by conformance suite
Exception hierarchy implementation - directly relevant to exception conformance tests; defines exception classes, matching, chaining, ExceptionGroup (PEP 654)
Generator state machine implementation - directly relevant to generator/iterator conformance tests; defines yield, send(), throw(), close() semantics
Class system and method dispatch - needed for custom exception subclassing and custom iterator protocol; defines MRO, super(), magic method dispatch
Built-in functions conformance - defines iterator builtins (enumerate, zip, reversed), aggregation builtins (min, max, sum, sorted), type checking (isinstance)
Overview of P0 features including builtins conformance verification strategy; provides context for which builtins are in scope for testing
Conductor integration context - shows how Mamba modules are wired; provides context for project structure and test execution environment
Pattern matching support - relevant for match/case statements in conformance test fixtures; provides context for Python 3.12 language features
Crate wiring and module registration - provides context for project-mode builds and native module integration used in test execution
Iterator protocol - relevant to generator/iterator conformance tests; defines __iter__ and __next__ dispatch for custom iterators
List operations - relevant to data structure conformance tests; defines list methods (append, extend, sort, slice, etc.)
Dict operations - relevant to data structure conformance tests; defines dict methods (get, update, keys, values, items, merge)
Set operations - relevant to data structure conformance tests; defines set algebra (union, intersection, difference)
Tuple operations - relevant to data structure conformance tests; defines tuple unpacking and comparison
String operations - relevant to data structure conformance tests; defines string methods (split, join, strip, replace, format)
